faq_1_question: What is Duty Drawback and how can it reduce my customs costs?
faq_1_answer: Duty Drawback is a U.S. Customs program (19 U.S.C. § 1313) that allows you to recover 99% of duties, taxes, and fees paid on imported goods that are later exported or destroyed. For retail brands, this means reclaiming costs on unsold inventory exported to consignees or customer returns shipped back overseas. It directly improves your landed cost margins and cash flow.
faq_2_question: Can non-US brands claim Duty Drawback on returned goods exported from the US?
faq_2_answer: Yes, if you meet three conditions: (1) You were the importer of record when the goods entered the US, (2) the returned goods are identical to the imported ones (or qualify under substitution rules), and (3) they are exported or destroyed within 5 years of import. Many non-US brands overlook this—returns can generate 6-8% cost savings on original import duties.
faq_3_question: How does Duty Drawback apply to US brands exporting inventory to overseas consignees?
faq_3_answer: If you import goods into the US and later export them unused to consignees (e.g., distributors, subsidiaries), you can claim drawback. Direct Identification (matching serial numbers) is simplest, but Substitution Drawback (using identical replacement goods) is more practical for retail. Pro tip: Track inventory by HTS code to maximize eligibility.
faq_4_question: What documentation is critical for Duty Drawback claims?
faq_4_answer:
Non-negotiable records: (1) CBP Form 7501 (entry summary) proving duties paid, (2) Commercial invoices and packing lists, (3) Proof of export (e.g., AES filings, bills of lading, or CBP Form 7512 for informal exports), and (4) Inventory tracking linking imports to exports. For returns, RMA documentation is also key. Digital records are acceptable but must be retrievable for 5+ years.

The TDR Advantage: Our team will provide all necessary support to create a compliant repository of documentation. With your consent, they will also access your ACE profile and pull down necessary data.
faq_5_question: How long does it take to receive Duty Drawback refunds?
faq_5_answer:
CBP processes claims in 3-12 months on average. Accelerated refunds can reduce this to 4-8 weeks if you're a trusted filer.

The TDR Advantage: Our team will request Accelerated Payment privilege on your behalf.

Finance teams: Plan for a 6-month working capital cycle—but refunds are interest-free, so the ROI is effectively the full duty amount recovered.
faq_6_question: What percentage of duties and fees can I recover?
faq_6_answer: 99% of duties, taxes, and most fees (e.g., Harbor Maintenance Fee, Merchandise Processing Fee). Exceptions: Some trade remedy fees (e.g., antidumping/countervailing duties) may have lower recovery rates or restrictions. Example: On $100,000 of duties paid, you'd recover $99,000—a direct boost to your P&L.
faq_7_question: What are the top reasons Duty Drawback claims get rejected?
faq_7_answer:
Avoid these mistakes: (1) Missing export proof (e.g., no AES filing for informal shipments), (2) Inadequate inventory tracking (can't link imports to exports), (3) Late filings (5-year deadline from import), (4) Incorrect HTS classification mismatches, and (5) Using non-identical substitution goods.

The TDR Advantage: Our team will support you implementing a drawback-specific ERP workflow before claiming.
faq_8_question: What's the difference between Direct Identification and Substitution Drawback?
faq_8_answer: Direct Identification: You export the exact same goods you imported (e.g., serialized inventory). Substitution: You export identical replacement goods (same HTS, value, and condition) when the originals are unavailable. Retail use case: Substitution is ideal for fungible goods (e.g., apparel, electronics) where tracking individual units is impractical. Manufacturing Drawback (19 U.S.C. § 1313(b)) is rarer but applies if you use imported components in exported products.
faq_9_question: Should we file Duty Drawback claims in-house or use a customs broker?
faq_9_answer: DIY: Feasible if you have an ABI software and robust customs expertise. Alternatively, TDR takes care of all the heavy lifting, including EDI connection with Customs, and typically increases recovery rate by 30% through optimization.
faq_10_question: Are there audit risks with Duty Drawback, and how can we mitigate them?
faq_10_answer:
Yes—CBP audits ~5% of claims annually. Red flags: High claim volumes, frequent substitution use, or inconsistent documentation. Mitigation: (1) Conduct a pre-filing audit of your records, (2) Use ACE Drawback module (CBP's preferred system), (3) Document everything (e.g., why substitution was used), and (4) Designate a single POC for CBP inquiries. Penalties for negligence can include denied claims or fines up to the duty amount.

The TDR Advantage: Trust our expert team to submit fully compliant claims to CBP and reduce the risk of Customs audits.
